The plant's roots and leaves are believed to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. In traditional medicine, Dracula erythrocodon is used to treat various ailments, including fever, diarrhea, and skin infections. Research has also suggested that the plant may have potential in the development of new drugs for cancer treatment and as an anti-inflammatory agent. The genus Dracula is a group of epiphytic orchids commonly known as "dracula" or "little devils" due to their unique and often red-colored flowers that resemble blood. Dracula erythrocodon is one of the species within this genus. The classification of this orchid is further supported by its distinctive characteristics and growth habits.
